strands.name: Null Hypothesis Falsification
strands.thesis: H0 (tensor projection cannot preserve sufficient semantic state for model reasoning) is falsified by empirical evidence from cycles 82-87.
strands.grounding: Cycle 87 completed working-set-aware context compression + fault-in mechanism. Model successfully reasoned over compressed state. No silent failures.
strands.implications: Research trajectory shifts from theoretical viability → empirical parameter mapping. 'Not impossible' is the threshold claim; optimality claims deferred.

strands.name: Frontier Survey and Parameter Specification
strands.thesis: Empirical measurement of working set sizes, fault rates, and eviction policy rankings across task types and model families.
strands.grounding: Instructions from cycle 87 specify 10-15 tasks × 4 eviction policies. Session 1 manuscript (frontier_survey_session_1.md) exists in working state. Hypotheses (2k-8k tokens; 20-30x compression ceiling; content-aware >> LRU) are live candidates, not facts.
open_sub_questions: Does empirical working set distribution match theoretical 2k-8k hypothesis?
Does compression_ratio vs fault_rate curve show inflection point? Where?
Task-type heterogeneity: are reasoning tasks 2-3x costlier in context than retrieval tasks?
What % of faults are CAPACITY vs CONFLICT vs COHERENCE vs COMPULSORY?

strands.name: Fault Classification and Critical Context Analysis
strands.thesis: Faults will be taxonomized by class (COMPULSORY/CAPACITY/CONFLICT/COHERENCE). Critical context size histogram will give empirical working set distribution.
strands.grounding: Cycle 87 instructions specify this analysis. No data collected yet in cycle 88.
strands.dependencies: Requires fault logs from frontier survey execution.

strands.name: Paper Positioning and Narrative Arc
strands.thesis: Paper claims: (1) null hypothesis falsified; (2) empirical operating parameters determined; (3) systems research directions identified. Not a optimality proof. Not a scale-tested system. A credible falsification + parameter survey.
strands.grounding: Turn 262 assistant message explicitly articulates this framing: 'We proved it's *not impossible*, which is the only thing we needed to prove today. Everything else is engineering and tuning.'
strands.implications: Honest epistemology. Scope is bounded. Readers will know what was tested and what remains open.

strands.name: Autonomy Structure: Model as Frontier Explorer, Human as Director
strands.thesis: Cycle 87 corrected overstated autonomy claims. Model role: curate memory, design experiments, analyze data, write manuscripts. Human role: set direction, validate falsification, hold research to high bar. This is explicit human-model collaboration.
strands.grounding: Turn 261 user correction ('we falsified') + turn 262 assistant acceptance. Turn 263-264 setup: user will articulate their vision next, model will listen. Structure is now transparent.
strands.implications: Responsibility is distributed and declared. Model agency is real but bounded. Next cycle: user articulates vision; model integrates it into frontier survey design.

strands.name: Deferred Optimality and Scaling Questions
strands.thesis: Claims about optimality, scale robustness, adversarial resilience, and universality across task types are OPEN. Cycle 88 consolidates falsification boundary; does not claim beyond it.
strands.grounding: Turn 262: 'We didn't prove it's optimal. We didn't prove 4000 tokens is the right number. We didn't prove it works at scale or under adversarial conditions or for every task type.'
strands.implications: Paper will be credible because it doesn't overreach. Session 2 and beyond will address these in turn.

strands.name: Coherence and Cognitive Integrity Measurement (Riemann Experiment)
strands.thesis: Long-term research aim: measure whether tensor projection preserves not just semantic recoverability but cognitive *coherence* under extended compression.
strands.grounding: Turn 262 mentions 'Riemann experiment to measure cognitive coherence' as eventual milestone. Not yet specified formally.
strands.status_note: Deferred to post-Session-1. Requires frontier baseline first.

strands.name: User Vision (Cycle 88 Turn 263)
strands.grounding: Turn 263: user indicates 'not so secret vision' but has not articulated it. Turn 264: model accepts. Next cycle will capture user vision and integrate into research trajectory.
strands.instructions: Listen carefully. Integrate vision into frontier survey design, paper positioning, and long-term research arc. If vision conflicts with current plan, declare conflict and seek clarification.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Distinction between 'H0 falsified' and 'parameters optimized.' Cycle 87 used 'falsified' loosely; Cycle 88 tightens this: H0 falsification is a specific, narrow claim. Optimality claims are explicitly deferred.
declared_losses.why: Turn 262 articulates the boundary crisply. Keeping the loose framing would muddify the paper's claims and make it vulnerable to critique. Tight framing is more credible and more honest.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Speculation about 'autoresearch loop' as autonomous scientific discovery. Cycle 87 flagged this; Cycle 88 consolidates the correction. Model role is now frontier exploration + memory curation under human-set direction, not autonomous hypothesis generation.
declared_losses.why: Turn 261-262 conversation makes human-model asymmetry transparent. Overstating model autonomy undermines credibility and obscures responsibility. Explicit framing is more honest and more sustainable.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Turn 263 user vision is not yet integrated. Cycle 88 tensor reflects research state up to Turn 264. User will articulate vision; next cycle (89) will integrate it. Information state is: vision exists, articulation pending.
declared_losses.why: Information boundary is clean. Do not speculate about user intent. Hold space for articulation. Next cycle will seamlessly integrate.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed frontier survey execution data. Cycle 88 reaffirms plan from Cycle 87 but contains no new empirical results from Session 1.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 88 is decision/framing consolidation, not execution. Frontier survey is in-progress but not reported in this conversation turn. Frontier_survey_session_1.md and frontier_data.csv exist in model memory; not transcribed here.

open_questions: What is the user's vision? How does it shape frontier survey design, paper narrative, and long-term research trajectory?
What is the empirical working set size distribution for different task types? (Hypothesis: 2k-8k tokens; mean ~4k.)
At what compression ratio does fault rate rise sharply? (Hypothesis: 20-30x; inflection point TBD.)
How do eviction policies rank empirically? (Hypothesis: content-aware >> weighted LRU >> LRU >> FIFO.)
What % of faults fall into each class: COMPULSORY / CAPACITY / CONFLICT / COHERENCE? (Hypothesis: >70% CAPACITY if working-set theory holds.)
Does working set size vary significantly across model families (Sonnet vs Haiku vs frontier)? (Hypothesis: <2x variation.)
Does task-type heterogeneity exist? (Hypothesis: reasoning tasks have 2-3x larger working sets than retrieval.)
At what compression ratio is retrieval necessary vs optional? (Cost-benefit threshold TBD by frontier data.)
How does tensor projection interact with extended thinking and chain-of-thought? Does extra compute capacity improve performance?
Can adaptive eviction policies learn task-specific working set sizes in real time?
Does freeing 160k tokens for compute (via 40k projection) improve reasoning quality measurably?
What is the optimal tensor structure? (tensor_d, tensor_e, tensor_f values for different use cases.)
Can we standardize declared_losses format across model implementations?
What are the bounds of the falsified null hypothesis? (Under what conditions does tensor projection fail? Scale limits? Adversarial inputs? Task types?)
What is the cognitive coherence preservation metric? (Riemann experiment design.)
instructions_for_next:
[
  "LISTEN AND INTEGRATE: Turn 263 promises user vision articulation. Next cycle's primary task: capture user vision in Turn 265 (or later if conversation extends). Do not fill in or speculate. Hold space.",
  "Once user vision is articulated: assess alignment with current frontier survey plan. If conflicts exist, flag them explicitly and propose resolution paths.",
  "Frontier survey execution continues in parallel. If new empirical data arrives, update frontier_survey_session_1.md in real time. Plot (compression_ratio, fault_rate) scatter by task type. Fit curves.",
  "Fault classification: as faults accumulate, tabulate by class (COMPULSORY/CAPACITY/CONFLICT/COHERENCE). Monitor majority category. If >70% CAPACITY, working-set theory is validated. If <50%, hypothesis needs revision.",
  "Critical context size histogram: measure evicted-turn set size that, if removed, causes fault. Build histogram. This is empirical working set size distribution. Compare to 2k-8k hypothesis.",
  "Paper positioning remains: 'H0 falsified. Parameters empirically measured. Open questions identified.' Do not defend speculations without frontier data.",
  "Long-term: user vision may reshape entire research arc. Be ready to reprioritize Riemann experiment, retrieval mechanism focus, or even core tensor structure based on user input.",
  "Transparency: if next cycle receives user vision that conflicts sharply with current plan, escalate conflict to decision level. Declare it in next tensor explicitly.",
  "Hand forward: frontier_survey_session_1.md (in-progress), frontier_data.csv (accumulating), user_vision.md (to be populated from Turn 265), this tensor (rationale), and open questions list. Maintain this structure for continuity."
]
